config.script:
# Define the folder path
	folderPath = "[default]_types_"
	
	# List to hold dropdown options
	options = []
	
	# Browse the folder for tags filtering just UDTTypes
	results = system.tag.browse(folderPath , {"tagType":"UdtType", "recursive":True})
	
	# Iterate through the results and collect tag names
	for result in results.getResults():
	    # Get the full path of the tag and convert it to a string
	    fullPath = str(result['fullPath'])
	    # Split the full path by '_types_' and get the last part (tag name)
	    tagName = fullPath.split('_types_/')[-1]  # Get the last element after splitting
	    # Create a dictionary for each tag for the dropdown
	    options.append({"label": tagName, "value": tagName})
	
	#Set the options property of the dropdown component
	self.props.options = options

# Get the data from the table (assuming it's a dataset)
	data = self.parent.parent.getChild("FlexContainer").getChild("Table").props.data
	
	# Initialize a set to hold unique values of the "Type" column
	unique_types = set()
	
	# Loop through each row in the table data
	for row in data:
	    type_value = row['Type']  # Replace 'Type' with the exact column name
	    unique_types.add(type_value)  # Add the type to the set, ensuring only unique values
	
	# Convert the unique types to a list of dictionaries for the dropdown options
	options = [{"label": type_value, "value": type_value} for type_value in unique_types]
	
	# Set the options property of the dropdown component
	dropdown = self.getSibling("Dropdown")  # Adjust the path to your actual dropdown component
	self.props.options = options

	folderPath = "[default]_types_"
	
	# List to hold dropdown options
	options = []
	
	# Browse the folder for tags filtering just UDTTypes
	results = system.tag.browse(folderPath , {"tagType":"UdtType", "recursive":True})
	
	# Iterate through the results and collect tag names
	for result in results.getResults():
	    # Get the full path of the tag and convert it to a string
	    fullPath = str(result['fullPath'])
	    # Split the full path by '_types_' and get the last part (tag name)
	    tagName = fullPath.split('_types_/')[-1]  # Get the last element after splitting

	    options.append({"label": tagName, "value": tagName})
	
	#Set the options property of the dropdown component
	self.props.options = options

# Get the data from the table (assuming it's a dataset)
	data = self.parent.parent.getChild("FlexContainer").getChild("Table").props.data
	
	# Initialize a set to hold unique values of the "Type" column
	unique_types = set()
	
	# Loop through each row in the table data
	for row in data:
	    type_value = row['Type']  # Replace 'Type' with the exact column name
	    unique_types.add(type_value)  # Add the type to the set, ensuring only unique values
	
	# Convert the unique types to a list of dictionaries for the dropdown options
	options = [{"label": type_value, "value": type_value} for type_value in unique_types]
	
	# Set the options property of the dropdown component
	dropdown = self.getSibling("Dropdown")  # Adjust the path to your actual dropdown component
	self.props.options = options

#clear udt selections
	self.parent.parent.getChild("Dropdown1").getChild("Dropdown0").props.value = 0
	self.parent.parent.getChild("Dropdown2").getChild("Dropdown0").props.value = 0
	self.parent.parent.getChild("Dropdown3").getChild("Dropdown0").props.value = 0
	self.parent.parent.getChild("Dropdown4").getChild("Dropdown0").props.value = 0
	self.parent.parent.getChild("Dropdown5").getChild("Dropdown0").props.value = 0
	self.parent.parent.getChild("Dropdown6").getChild("Dropdown0").props.value = 0
	self.parent.parent.getChild("Dropdown7").getChild("Dropdown0").props.value = 0
	self.parent.parent.getChild("Dropdown8").getChild("Dropdown0").props.value = 0
	self.parent.parent.getChild("Dropdown9").getChild("Dropdown0").props.value = 0
	self.parent.parent.getChild("Dropdown10").getChild("Dropdown0").props.value = 0
	#clear tag selections
	self.parent.parent.getChild("Dropdown1").getChild("Dropdown").props.value = 0
	self.parent.parent.getChild("Dropdown2").getChild("Dropdown").props.value = 0
	self.parent.parent.getChild("Dropdown3").getChild("Dropdown").props.value = 0
	self.parent.parent.getChild("Dropdown4").getChild("Dropdown").props.value = 0
	self.parent.parent.getChild("Dropdown5").getChild("Dropdown").props.value = 0
	self.parent.parent.getChild("Dropdown6").getChild("Dropdown").props.value = 0
	self.parent.parent.getChild("Dropdown7").getChild("Dropdown").props.value = 0
	self.parent.parent.getChild("Dropdown8").getChild("Dropdown").props.value = 0
	self.parent.parent.getChild("Dropdown9").getChild("Dropdown").props.value = 0
	self.parent.parent.getChild("Dropdown10").getChild("Dropdown").props.value = 0

# Get the pasted data from the Text Area
	pastedData = self.getSibling("TextArea").props.text  # Adjust for the actual Text Area component path
	
	# Split pasted data into rows
	rows = pastedData.split("\n")
	
	# Initialize an empty list to hold the table data
	tableData = []
	
	# Iterate through rows
	for row in rows:
	    if row.strip():  # Ensure the row is not empty
	        # Split each row by tab (adjust to commas if CSV)
	        columns = row.split("\t")  # Adjust delimiter depending on the pasted data format
	        # Create a dictionary for each row (adjust keys to match your table's column names)
	        rowData = {
	            "Type": columns[0],  # Adjust column names as per your table's columns
	            "Tag": columns[1]
	            
	            # Add more columns as needed
	        }
	        # Append the row data to the list
	        tableData.append(rowData)
	
	# Assign the parsed data to the table's props.data property
	self.parent.parent.getChild("Table").props.data = tableData  # Adjust for the actual Table component path

#Delete Folders and all Nested Tags
	results = system.tag.browse("[default]")

	for i in results.getResults():
	    stringPath = str(i['fullPath'])
	    system.tag.deleteTags([stringPath])
	    
	#Refresh Tag Tree
	self.parent.parent.parent.getChild("FlexContainer").getChild("TagBrowseTree").props.root.path = " "
	self.parent.parent.parent.getChild("FlexContainer").getChild("TagBrowseTree").props.root.path = ""

tableData = self.parent.parent.parent.getChild("FlexContainer").getChild("Table").props.data
	param1Name = self.parent.parent.parent.getChild("Parameter").getChild("Parameter1Name").props.text
	param1 = self.parent.parent.parent.getChild("Parameter").getChild("Parameter1").props.text
	param2Name = self.parent.parent.parent.getChild("Parameter2").getChild("Parameter2Name").props.text
	param2 = self.parent.parent.parent.getChild("Parameter2").getChild("Parameter2").props.text
	
	# Create lists to store the selections and selected types
	selections = []
	selectedTypes = []
	
	# Loop through dropdowns 1 to 10
	for i in range(1, 11):
	    dropdown = self.parent.parent.parent.getChild("Dropdown{}".format(i)).getChild("Dropdown0").props.value
	    selections.append(dropdown)
	    
	    selectedType = self.parent.parent.parent.getChild("Dropdown{}".format(i)).getChild("Dropdown").props.value
	    selectedTypes.append(selectedType)
	
	for i in range (0,10):
		#Add Folders if selection not Null
		if selections[i]:
			folderName = selections[i].replace("/" , "_")
			system.tag.addTag(parentPath='', name=folderName, tagType="Folder")
		#Check for blank entries, then call GnenerateTag
		if selections[i] !0:
			if selectedTypes[i] !0:
				folderName = selections[i].replace("/" , "_")
				GenerateTag.generateTag(tableData, selectedTypes[i], selections[i], folderName, param1Name, param1, param2Name, param2)	
	#Refresh Tag Tree
	self.parent.parent.parent.getChild("FlexContainer").getChild("TagBrowseTree").props.root.path = " "
	self.parent.parent.parent.getChild("FlexContainer").getChild("TagBrowseTree").props.root.path = ""

#Exports Tags to Gateway
	filePath = 'C:\\tags.json'        
	tagPaths = ["[default]"]
	recursive = True
 
	system.tag.exportTags(filePath, tagPaths, recursive, format="JSON")
	
	#Download file from Gateway
	filename = 'tags.json'
	data = system.file.readFileAsBytes('C:\\'+filename)
	system.perspective.download(filename, data)
